windows.embed_text_v2: Showing sinkhole defects beside wicks in set cream soy candles in glass jars, cooled wax shrinkage craters demonstrated, then pouring wax to fill the sinkhole, candle making troubleshooting

tags_v2.action: showing sinkhole defects and filling them

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead close-up of two set cream-yellow soy candles in glass jars on dark wood; each has a trimmed white wick and a small brown sinkhole crater in the wax surface beside the wick.
Same two jar candles from above, the cooled pale wax showing shrinkage dips and small sinkhole holes near each wick, illustrating a candle-making defect.
Camera tilts to show the two glass-jar candles edge-on plus a third out-of-focus jar behind; the cured wax surfaces have visible sinkhole pits next to the wicks.
Tight macro on a single jar candle with a burned-in '2. Sink hole' caption; a clear brown sinkhole cavity sits beside the short white wick in the cream wax.
Same macro of the sinkhole defect, '2. Sink hole' overlay text top-center, the crater and wick centered in the glass.
Continued close view of the sinkhole next to the wick, '2. Sink hole' caption still shown, highlighting the wax shrinkage flaw.
The sinkhole and wick remain framed with the '2. Sink hole' label, demonstrating the imperfection in the set candle.
Final second: macro of a jar candle as pale liquid wax is poured in to fill the sinkhole cavity beside the wick, a second top-up pour, jar resting on a knitted mat.

seconds_v2.desc: Close-up of two amber glass jar candles with set cream-colored wax; their white wicks are held upright, tied to a blue pen laid across the rim, and a small sinkhole shows beside the front wick, jars sitting on a chunky knit blanket.
Same two amber jars, wicks centered by the blue pen resting on the rims; the front candle's cream wax surface has a brown sinkhole pit near the wick.
Slightly shifted view of the pair of amber jar candles, white wicks suspended from the blue pen across the tops, set wax showing shrinkage holes.
Tighter angle on the two amber jars, the blue pen and a wooden board behind; wicks held straight as the wax has cooled with sinkhole dips.
A motion-blurred transition swings past the amber jar candles with their suspended wicks toward a new shot.
Macro inside an empty stainless steel pouring/melting pot with a black handle, its shiny interior showing embossed 'MAX' and 'MIN' fill-level marks, set on a wood surface.
Same empty steel melting pot interior, the 'MIN' and 'MAX' embossed lines visible, ready for wax to be added.
Final second: continued close view of the clean stainless steel pouring pot with its 'MAX'/'MIN' marks and black handle, empty before melting wax.

Molten cream wax streams from the steel jug into the front candle jar for the second pour, the '6. SECOND POUR' overlay still shown, a blue glue-stick lying at left.
The second pour continues filling the front jar's surface, both wicks held straight by the metal centering bars, '6. SECOND POUR' caption present.
Portrait-cropped close-up (black side bars): the steel pitcher pours a thin layer of cream wax over the set candle in the front jar, wick held by the slotted metal bar.
Pouring continues, the fresh wax leveling the top of the front candle, second jar beside it with its own centered wick, blue applicator at left.
The top-up wax spreads smooth across the front jar surface as the pitcher tips, both wicks upright in their metal holders.
More molten wax is added to finish the front candle's top, the pour stream visible from the steel jug, jars on the dark wood.
Final second: the second pour finishes filling the front jar with a smooth cream layer, wick centered, pitcher still tilted above.

seconds_v2.desc: Two already-set white soy candles in clear glass jars with metal wick-centering bars across the rims sit on a dark wood surface; a small metal pouring jug enters frame at top right; a white squeeze bottle with blue cap lies on left.

Pouring jug tips slightly back into frame above the rear left jar.
Pouring jug positioned over the left jar; a thin stream of melted wax begins falling into the rear jar for a second top-up pour.
Continuous thin stream of clear molten wax pouring from the jug into the back jar, building a thin layer over the previously set white wax.
Pour continues into the rear jar; wax surface in jar smoothing out under the fresh pour.
Pouring jug still tilted, thin glossy stream of wax delivering the second pour to fill the sinkhole on the rear candle.

seconds_v2.desc: Portrait-cropped close-up (black side bars): a steel pitcher finishes a second pour of cream wax into the front of two glass jar candles on dark wood, both wicks held by slotted metal centering bars, a blue glue-stick at left.
The last of the cream wax is poured into the front jar, the two candles' surfaces now level, wicks upright in their metal holders.
Pour complete; the two filled jar candles sit with smooth cream tops, wicks held straight by the metal bars across the rims.
Wider set candles, now cooled, the metal bars removed; two jars show long upright wicks, a third frosted-white candle with pink swirl sits behind at right.
A bare hand holds the front candle's wick taut while a pair of scissors moves in to trim it; the back jar's wick stands tall, only fingers and forearm visible (no face).
The hand steadies the wick as the scissors close around it to cut it to length above the set cream wax in the front jar.
The front wick has been trimmed short and neat; both jar candles now show set cream surfaces, the swirled candle still at right.
Final second: a hand brings scissors to trim the rear jar's wick, the front candle already finished with a short centered wick, both on the dark wood table.
